results.text:
Tailwind CSS v4.1: Text shadows, masks, and tons more - Tailwind CSS

v4.3

April 3, 2025

# Tailwind CSS v4.1: Text shadows, masks, and tons more

Adam Wathan

Dan Hollick

I wasn't sure it would ever happen but we did it — we released a version of Tailwind CSS that includes`text-shadow` utilities.

Tailwind CSS v4.1 is here and it's packed with new utilities, variants, and developer experience improvements that will help you (or your LLM, you coward) build even better interactive experiences.

Here's all the best stuff we got into this release:

- New`text-shadow-*` utilities — only about twenty years after they were first supported by a browser.
- Mask elements with the`mask-*` utilities — use images and gradients to mask elements with new ergonomic APIs.
- Improved compatibility with older browsers — we've made things degrade way more gracefully for older browsers, while still taking advantage of modern features in browsers that support them.
- Fine-grained text wrapping with`overflow-wrap`— defend the integrity of your layouts from even the longest German words your users will throw at you.
- Colored`drop-shadow` support — can't really remember why we didn't have these before but we do now.
- Target input devices with`pointer-*` and`any-pointer-*`— tweak your design for touch devices explicitly instead of relying on viewport size.
- Align items to the last baseline — align flex or grid items to the baseline of the last line of text using the new`items-baseline-last` and`self-baseline-last` utilities.
- Keep content visible with`safe` alignment — center content in flex and grid layouts without it disappearing when there's not enough space.
- Ignore specific paths with`@source not`— explicitly ignore irrelevant large directories and speed up your builds even more.
- Safelist specific utilities with`@source inline(…)`— force Tailwind to include classes that aren't in your source files.
- A bunch of other new variants — including`noscript`,`user-valid`,`inverted-colors`, and more.

That's all the cool stuff, but there's a few other little things hiding in the release notes that you might want to check out too.

Upgrade your projects by installing the latest version of`tailwindcss` from npm:

Using the Tailwind CLI

```
npm install tailwindcss@latest @tailwindcss/cli@latest
```

Using Vite

```
npm install tailwindcss@latest @tailwindcss/vite@latest
```

Using PostCSS

```
npm install tailwindcss@latest @tailwindcss/postcss@latest
```

---

## New text-shadow-* utilities

We've been threatening to add text shadows for at least the last six years and today they are finally here.

We've added five text shadows to the default theme, from`text-shadow-2xs` to`text-shadow-lg`. They are particularly useful for making headings stand out against a busy background:

The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.

The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.

The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.

The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.

The quick

results.title: Tailwind CSS v4.0
results.text:
Tailwind CSS v4.0 - Tailwind CSS

v4.3

January 22, 2025

# Tailwind CSS v4.0

Adam Wathan

Holy shit it's actually done — we just tagged Tailwind CSS v4.0.

Tailwind CSS v4.0 is an all-new version of the framework optimized for performance and flexibility, with a reimagined configuration and customization experience, and taking full advantage of the latest advancements the web platform has to offer.

- New high-performance engine — where full builds are up to 5x faster, and incremental builds are over 100x faster — and measured in microseconds.
- Designed for the modern web — built on cutting-edge CSS features like cascade layers, registered custom properties with`@property`, and`color-mix()`.
- Simplified installation — fewer dependencies, zero configuration, and just a single line of code in your CSS file.
- First-party Vite plugin — tight integration for maximum performance and minimum configuration.
- Automatic content detection — all of your template files are discovered automatically, with no configuration required.
- Built-in import support — no additional tooling necessary to bundle multiple CSS files.
- CSS-first configuration — a reimagined developer experience where you customize and extend the framework directly in CSS instead of a JavaScript configuration file.
- CSS theme variables — all of your design tokens exposed as native CSS variables so you can access them anywhere.
- Dynamic utility values and variants — stop guessing what values exist in your spacing scale, or extending your configuration for things like basic data attributes.
- Modernized P3 color palette — a redesigned, more vivid color palette that takes full advantage of modern display technology.
- Container queries — first-class APIs for styling elements based on their container size, no plugins required.
- New 3D transform utilities — transform elements in 3D space directly in your HTML.
- Expanded gradient APIs — radial and conic gradients, interpolation modes, and more.
- @starting-style support — a new variant you can use to create enter and exit transitions, without the need for JavaScript.
- not-* variant — style an element only when it doesn't match another variant, custom selector, or media or feature query.
- Even more new utilities and variants — including support for`color-scheme`,`field-sizing`, complex shadows,`inert`, and more.

Start using Tailwind CSS v4.0 today by installing it in a new project, or playing with it directly in the browser on Tailwind Play.

For existing projects, we've published a comprehensive upgrade guide and built an automated upgrade tool to get you on the latest version as quickly and painlessly as possible.

---

## New high-performance engine

Tailwind CSS v4.0 is a ground-up rewrite of the framework, taking everything we've learned about the architecture over the years and optimizing it to be as fast as possible.

When benchmarking it on our own projects, we've found full rebuilds to be over 3.5x faster, and incremental build

parts.text:
Based on the latest information, here's a comprehensive cheatsheet for **Tailwind CSS v4**: --- ## 🚀 **Tailwind v4 Cheatsheet** ### **Major Changes** - ✅ **No more `tailwind.config.js`** - Configuration now in CSS via `@theme` - ✅ **5x faster builds** with new Rust-based Oxide engine - ✅ **Automatic content detection** - no manual file path configuration - ✅ **CSS-first configuration** using CSS variables - ✅ **Built-in import support** - no extra bundling needed --- ## 📦 **Installation** ```bash # Vite npm install tailwindcss@latest @tailwindcss/vite@latest # PostCSS (Next.js, etc.) npm install tailwindcss@latest @tailwindcss/postcss@latest # CLI npm install tailwindcss@latest @tailwindcss/cli@latest ``` **CSS Entry Point:** ```css /* Before (v3) */ @tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities; /* After (v4) */ @import "tailwindcss"; ``` --- ## 🎨 **CSS-First Configuration with @theme** ```css @import "tailwindcss"; @theme { /* Colors (OKLCH for better color accuracy) */ --color-primary: oklch(60% 0.2 250); --color-primary-foreground: white; --color-brand-500: oklch(70% 0.15 240); /* Typography */ --font-sans: "Inter Variable", system-ui, sans-serif; --font-display: "Poppins", sans-serif; /* Spacing */ --spacing-18: 4.5rem; --spacing-88: 22rem; /* Border radius */ --radius: 0.5rem; --radius-card: 12px; /* Breakpoints */ --breakpoint-xs: 480px; --breakpoint-3xl: 1920px; /* Shadows */ --shadow-card: 0 4px 24px oklch(0% 0 0 / 0.1); /* Animations */ --animate-shimmer: shimmer 2s linear infinite; --keyframes-shimmer: { to { background-position: 200% center; } }; } ``` --- ## 🆕 **New Utilities** ### **Text Shadows** (v4.1+) ```html

Subtle shadow

Extra small

Small

Default

Large

``` ### **Mask Utilities** (v4.1+) ```html
Gradient mask
Radial mask
Custom mask
``` ### **3D Transforms** ```html
3D rotation
3D translation
``` ### **Container Queries** (First-Class) ```html
Responsive to sidebar width
``` ### **Overflow Wrap** (v4.1+) ```html

Normal wrapping

Break long words

Break anywhere

``` ### **Colored Drop Shadows** ```html
Red shadow
``` ### **Expanded Gradients** ```html
``` --- ## 🎯 **New Variants** ### **Nth-child Selectors** ```html
  • Zebra striping
  • Edge items
    • ``` ### **not-* Variant** ```html
    Not active
    ``` ### **@starting-style** (enter/exit animations) ```html
    Fades in on mount without JavaScript
    ``` ### **Field Sizing** ```html ``` ### **Input Device Detection** (v4.1+) ```html
    Has precision pointer
    ``` ### **Other New Variants** (v4.1+) ```html
    No JavaScript fallback
    User validation
    Inverted colors mode
    ``` ### **Baseline Alignment** (v4.1+) ```html
    Align to last baseline
    Self align to last baseline
    ``` ### **Safe Alignment** (v4.1+) ```html
    Won't overflow container
    ``` --- ## 🔧 **CSS Variables in Utilities** ```html
    Theme tokens
    Custom property
    CSS variable
    ``` --- ## 🎭 **Custom Variants** ```css /* Define custom variants */ @custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark *)); @custom-variant hocus (&:is(:hover, :focus)); ``` --- ## 📁 **Source Control** (v4.1+) ```css /* Ignore specific paths */ @source not "node_modules/**"; @source not "dist/**"; /* Safelist utilities not in source */ @source inline("flex items-center justify-center"); ``` --- ## 🌈 **Modernized Color Palette** v4 uses **OKLCH color space** for perceptually uniform colors: - Better color accuracy across devices - P3 wide-gamut display support - More vivid colors ```css @theme { --color-blue-500: oklch(60% 0.2 250); } ``` --- ## ⚡ **Performance Improvements** - **5x faster** full rebuilds - **100x faster** incremental builds (measured in microseconds) - Automatic content detection (no manual path config) - Built on Rust-based Oxide engine --- ## 🔄 **Migration Quick Reference** | v3 | v4 | |---|---| | `tailwind.config.js` | `@theme` in CSS | | `@tailwind base` | `@import "tailwindcss"` | | `darkMode: 'class'` | `@custom-variant dark` | | `content: [...]` | Automatic detection | | Plugin for containers | Built-in `@container` | --- This cheatsheet covers the major new features! For full migration guides, check the [official v4 documentation](https://tailwindcss.com/blog/tailwindcss-v4).
